rationale: The definition uses two distinct model notions: 'every model of T' (def:model-theory-set, present) and 'is a model of phi' where phi is a single sentence (def:model, Model of a Sentence) -- the latter was missing. T as a set of sentences is def:theory (present).
notes: Counter-reading: 'model of phi' could be deemed 'model of the singleton {phi}', i.e. def:model-theory-set applied to {phi}, making def:model redundant. But the text writes 'a model of phi' with phi a sentence, which is exactly def:model, and that node exists, so it is added. Resolves the 0014 thread: log-consequence reaches satisfaction transitively via def:model -> def:truth-structure -> def:fol-satisfaction.
